paper_title: Particle Size Measurements and Field Observations in the Miaoli Tableland

methods.description: Process for conducting particle size analyses using a laser diffractometer to measure fine-grained particles smaller than 2 mm.

steps.description: For each sample, ca. 20-50 mg of the sediments was filled into the test tubes and treated with 1% ammonium hydroxide (NH4OH) solution, in order to disperse the aggregates of sediments for the subsequent particle size analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sediment samples with ammonium hydroxide solution for dispersion.

steps.description: The test tubes were rotated for 24 h at 30 rpm to mix the solution and sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: Rotate test tubes to mix sediment and solution.
inputs: test tubes with sediment and solution

steps.description: The particle size was measured five times for each sample to reduce the random error.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure particle size multiple times to ensure accuracy.

steps.description: The results were accepted when the value of polarization intensity differential scattering (> 80%) and obscuration (< 10%) passed both criteria.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate measurements based on scattering and obscuration criteria.

steps.description: The statistical index of the coefficient of variance mean (< 5%) and coefficient of variance standard variation (< 5%) were chosen to evaluate the reproducibility of the results.
The particle size fractions and the texture classifications were correlated for the characterization of sedimentary records.
steps.searchable_summary: Correlate particle size fractions with texture classifications.

methods.description: Method for studying the internal sedimentological composition of the tableland segments to identify typical layer sequences.

steps.description: 51 field sites were chosen for a detailed description by the following criteria: the sites are (1) easily accessible and provide a good overview of the sedimentary layers, (2) evenly distributed across the study area, and (3) contain more than two visible sedimentary layers for a representative sampling and comparison with other outcrops.
steps.searchable_summary: Select field sites based on accessibility, distribution, and layer visibility.

steps.description: The elevations of the sites were extracted from the DEMs and compared with a handhold GPS receiver in the field.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract and verify site elevations using DEMs and GPS.

steps.description: The sedimentary layers in the outcrops were recorded by standard characteristics: shape of boundaries, thickness, sedimentary structures, texture, particle size, and visible fossil content.
steps.searchable_summary: Record sedimentary layer characteristics in the field.

steps.description: At 12 selected sites, 41 samples were taken for particle size analyses including 2 samples from modern beach (dune) sand for comparison.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect samples for particle size analysis.
